$comment: Project Claude Code settings for the dev-bootstrap framework. Wires the context guard (statusline monitor + PreCompact backstop + post-compact restore + checkpoint nudge) and a permission policy tuned for autonomous execution between human-decision checkpoints. Adjust the allow/deny lists to your stack and risk tolerance.

permissions.$comment_allow: Safe, routine actions the autonomous loop runs constantly. Allowing them means the framework doesn't stop to ask permission for normal work — which is the point. Tune to your stack.
permissions.$comment_ask: Actions worth a quick confirm but not outright dangerous. Merge is HERE, not in allow: in a solo repo, auto-merge deadlocks against branch protection's required review (you can't approve your own PR), so a human (or your CI-as-approver setup) closes the loop. See FRAMEWORK.md 'The merge gate'.
permissions.$comment_deny: Destructive / irreversible. The framework escalates these to a human regardless.
